commented_code:
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Generate a Super Bowl merch redemption URL.
+
+Prints only the final URL; no browser/open calls.
+"""
+
+import base64
+import hashlib
+import hmac
+import sys
+import time
+import urllib.parse
+
+SECRET_KEY = "dfc92bc5e95825103283f01c2aa6ca7fe7f6ffc31778ea82c354785c73b0858c"

discussion_comments.comment_body:
Yes this looks intentional, SECRET_KEY HMAC-signs {ts}:{email} so the server can verify the token wasn’t tampered with.

Since it’s public, the real gating is the timestamp expiry / server-side checks (and likely key rotation after the promo).
